question.text: In the context of derivative products, who pays the premium?

answer.text: The buyer of an option pays premium to the seller
The buyer of a futures contract pays premium to the exchange
Both parties in a forward contract pay premium to each other
The seller of an option pays premium to the buyer

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Premium is a concept specific to options. The option buyer pays a premium to the option seller (writer) for acquiring the right (but not obligation) to buy or sell the underlying. Futures and forwards do not involve premium payments. **The Trap:** Futures involve margins, not premiums. Forwards have no upfront payments. The option seller receives the premium — they do not pay it. **Cross-Exam Context:** Understanding premium is essential for NISM Series VIII and XIII (Equity Derivatives and Common Derivatives). **Key Takeaway:** Premium exists only in options. Buyer pays premium (cost of the right). Seller receives premium (compensation for taking on the obligation).
